topic_title: When NOT to use agents — high-stakes or irreversible actions require human oversight

14_9_defer_check.form: Section 11 (Next Steps) defers to 14.9 by name in one sentence: 'Topic 14.9 covers production AI patterns — cost, latency, and reliability trade-offs...' — name-only defer, acceptable

eu_ai_act_nist_rmf_check.note: Both appear in topic 14.8 cross_refs in sequence.index.json. Used as one-sentence supporting citations, not taught in depth. Acceptable.

length_sanity.note: Estimated ~4,020 words; comfortably within standard corpus range 3000–4400.

detail.headline: When NOT to use agents — high-stakes or irreversible actions require human oversight
detail.implementation_section: Describes logical/conceptual structure of a checkpoint pattern only. No code. Explicitly defers implementation to 'Semester 2 work.' Appropriate.
detail.eu_ai_act_nist_rmf: Referenced as cross_refs for this topic; used as one-liner supporting citations for human oversight argument — not taught as regulatory compliance content.
detail.anthropic_rsp: One sentence naming Anthropic's responsible scaling policy as supporting evidence. Acceptable mention-and-cite.

findings.description: Section 5.4 introduces four named positions on the autonomy spectrum (fully manual, human-in-the-loop, human-on-the-loop, fully autonomous) in quick succession. Each has its own labeled paragraph with a definition and example, so this is NOT a jargon dump within a single paragraph. No action required, but the reading artifact should pace these definitions carefully.

detail.plain_register: Consistent direct address ('you'), active voice, concrete examples before abstract definitions throughout.
detail.hitl_expansion: HITL spelled out as 'human-in-the-loop (HITL)' on first use in §4. Pass.
detail.rag_expansion: RAG spelled out as 'RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)' in prerequisites. Pass.
detail.nist_expansion: NIST AI Risk Management Framework given in full on first use in §5.2. Pass.
detail.escalation_rule: Only 1 soft finding in check 6 — does not meet the ≥3 distinct findings escalation threshold. Remains soft.

soft_findings: autonomy_spectrum_term_density: Section 5.4 introduces four named positions (fully manual, human-in-the-loop, human-on-the-loop, fully autonomous) in close succession — each is separately defined and exemplified, so not a jargon dump, but the reading artifact builder should pace these carefully.
verdict: PASS — all 6 checks pass. All 5 primary resources cited throughout (dense coverage in §5–§8). No forward references. ~4,020 words within standard corpus range (3000–4400). No scope creep: implementation is conceptual-only with an explicit Semester 2 defer, and EU AI Act / NIST RMF are listed cross_refs used as one-sentence supporting citations only. Beginner readability is strong; one soft note on the autonomy spectrum's four terms in §5.4 (paced across separate paragraphs — not a jargon dump). Corpus is ready to ground downstream artifacts.
